claimText: Under the EIA Notification 2006, Category A projects are handled by the MoEF&CC at the Central level, while Category B projects are handled by the SEIAA at the State level.
evidenceExcerpt: Projects are categorized into Category A (Central level, MoEF&CC) and Category B (State level, SEIAA).
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question swapping the authorities for Category A and Category B projects.
distractorHooks: Claiming both are handled by the Central Government
Claiming Category A is handled by NGT

claimText: The Supreme Court ruled in the Common Cause v. UoI (2017) case that ex post facto or retrospective environmental clearance is invalid and alien to environmental jurisprudence.
evidenceExcerpt: Supreme Court held in Common Cause v. UoI (2017) that ex post facto environmental clearance is invalid.
examUse: Statement-based question on whether retrospective environmental clearance is legally permissible in India.
distractorHooks: Stating ex post facto clearance is allowed with a penalty
Attributing the ruling to the National Green Tribunal (NGT) instead of the Supreme Court

claimText: The EIA Notification 2006 mandates prior Environmental Clearance for over 39 listed activities.
evidenceExcerpt: EIA Notification 2006 lists over 39 activities requiring prior EC.
examUse: Statement-based question testing if prior clearance is mandatory and the governing notification.
distractorHooks: Stating clearance can be obtained after project commencement
Citing the Environment Protection Act 1986 directly instead of the EIA Notification 2006
